keyPoints: Bar plots are more suitable for categorical data, while stacked bar charts are better for continuous data.
X-axis represents the satisfaction level, and the y-axis represents the repeat purchases.
To create a stacked bar chart, you need to have at least two groups (satisfaction levels) in the data.
Focusing on the top and bottom slice of the stacked bar chart can help identify the most satisfied and dissatisfied customers, respectively.
By comparing the heights of the bars in a bar plot or stacked bar chart, you can identify trends and patterns in the data.
